payload.prompt: Look at my progress/${username}.progress.json. Suggest the next topic I should learn. Prefer in-progress leaves over not-started ones; prefer leaves whose prerequisite siblings are done. Give me two concrete options and let me pick — do not start the explanation yet.

labels.en: Quiz me on the current topic

payload.prompt: Use the quiz-generator skill to build a 4-9 question multiple-choice quiz on the topic I am currently working through (the deepest in-progress leaf in my progress file). Ground every question in a wiki page or RAG passage; never invent facts. Render the quiz as an MUI-React HTML page under out/quizzes/<topic-slug>.quiz.html and emit a <preview:out/quizzes/<topic-slug>.quiz.html> tag so it opens in the preview pane.

Ask me which tool I want to practice (SAP MD04, a CRM mock, an ERP form). If a simulator already exists under out/simulators/ for that tool, open it via the render_simulator MCP tool and emit a <preview:out/simulators/<app>.simulator.html> tag. If not, invoke the simulator-author skill to generate one. Coach me step by step using the click stream that arrives via viewerState — one step at a time, don't dump the whole expected sequence at me. If I get stuck for 30 s, call highlight_simulator_step to flash the next hot-spot.

labels.en: Explain something to me as if I'm new
labels.de: Erkläre mir etwas, als wäre ich neu
labels.it: Spiegami qualcosa come se fossi nuovo
labels.zh: 把某事讲得像我是新人一样

payload.prompt: Ask me what topic I want explained. Then explain it adapted to my baseline (language preference + prior knowledge stored in progress/${username}.progress.json). Bring up at most one supporting artifact in the preview pane (a wiki page, RAG document, or example file). End with one quick check-question I can answer to lock the concept in.

labels.en: Curate today's additions to the wiki
labels.it: Cura le aggiunte di oggi al wiki
labels.zh: 整理今天对维基的补充

payload.prompt: Walk through every document added to documents/ or inbox/ in the last 24 hours. For each one, propose: which wiki page(s) it strengthens, which it makes stale, and any new wiki page that should exist. Surface a punch list — do not edit the wiki yet. End by asking me which proposals to apply.

labels.en: Show wiki coverage vs. the curriculum
labels.it: Mostra la copertura del wiki rispetto al curriculum
labels.zh: 显示维基对课程的覆盖情况

payload.prompt: Compare the curriculum ToC in progress/_template.progress.json against the wiki/ directory. List every ToC leaf with no matching wiki page, every wiki page with no ToC leaf, and every leaf whose wiki page is marked 'stub' or 'draft'. This is the coverage gap a guest would hit.

labels.en: Add a topic to the curriculum

payload.prompt: Ask me which top-level section the new topic belongs under (1-5). Propose a slug + title + 1-paragraph wiki stub. After I confirm, write the wiki page under wiki/topics/<slug>.md AND add the leaf to progress/_template.progress.json (so every future guest inherits the new structure). Existing guests' progress files are not modified — that's their decision.

Ask me which application I want a simulator for (e.g. JIRA, Polarion, CANoe, a CRM, an ERP form) and what 3 click sequences matter most. Use the simulator-author skill to generate out/simulators/<app-id>.simulator.html — three screens max, six hot-spots max per screen, EXPECTED_STEPS array filled in. Show me a draft before writing the file; I confirm or correct. After I confirm, write the file and emit a <preview:...> so I can sanity-check by clicking through it myself.

labels.it: Rivedi le domande aperte dei tirocinanti
labels.zh: 查看学员的未解决问题

payload.prompt: Scan every progress/<username>.progress.json under progress/. List Q/A entries where the answer was marked unconfirmed, contradicted the wiki, or generated a 'clarify' state. Surface these as a queue with username, topic, question, and my draft answer. I will review and confirm — your draft does not become canon until I sign off.
